title: China scraps tariffs for all but one African nation

title: Man sentenced to death for murder of toddlers at Ugandan nursery
content: Christopher Okello Onyum had pleaded insanity but this was dismissed by the judge.

originalTitle: Trump expands red snapper fishing as critics warn of overfishing
title: Trump expands red snapper fishing as critics warn of overfishing

originalTitle: At least 12 killed in latest Israeli attacks on Lebanon
title: At least 12 killed in latest Israeli attacks on Lebanon

originalTitle: Iranian Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi hospitalised as health deteriorates
title: A Brave Lady Needs Our Wishes: Narges Mohammadi is unwell
content:
Hello from Your Kid-Friendly News Reporter in Africa!

Hi everyone! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you important news from around the world. We live in a big world, and sometimes we hear about brave people who do wonderful things. Today, we're talking about a very special lady. Her name is Narges Mohammadi.

Who is Narges Mohammadi?

Narges Mohammadi is a super brave lady from a country called Iran. That's very far away from many parts of Africa, but we care about everyone! She is known for helping people. She wants everyone to be treated fairly and with kindness. She believes everyone should be safe and happy. She helps people by speaking up when things are not fair. This takes a lot of courage, just like when you're brave enough to try something new, like riding a bike without training wheels!

What is the Nobel Peace Prize?

Guess what? Narges Mohammadi won a super, super special award! It's called the Nobel Peace Prize. Wow! Did you know? The Nobel Peace Prize is one of the most important awards in the whole world! People get it for working hard to make our world more peaceful and kind. They help stop fighting and make sure everyone can live happily together. Think about it like getting a gold star for being the kindest person in your class, but for the whole world! She got this prize because she works so hard to make sure people are treated well and can speak freely.

Why is She So Special?

Narges believes that everyone should have a voice. She thinks it's important for people to be able to say what they think, especially if they see something that is not fair. She wants all people, especially women and girls, to be strong and have equal chances. She has spent many years working to help others. She teaches people about their rights. She shows everyone that being kind and fair is the best way to live.

What Happened to Narges Mohammadi?

Now for some news that makes us a little sad. Narges Mohammadi has been feeling very unwell. She got very, very sick and needed to go to a special place where doctors and nurses can help her. That special place is called a hospital. Hospitals are places full of amazing doctors and nurses who work hard to help people when they are sick or hurt.

What Does 'Lost Consciousness' Mean?

The news told us that Narges fell asleep suddenly, twice, and couldn't wake up for a little while. This is called 'losing consciousness.' It means her body needed a big rest because she was so unwell. It's like when you're super tired and just nod off, but much more serious. When this happens, it means your body is telling you it needs a lot of help and care.

Her Heart Needed Help

The news also said her heart had a big problem. This is called a 'severe cardiac crisis.' Our hearts are super important! They pump blood all around our body, helping us run, jump, and even think. Did you know? Your heart is about the size of your fist! It works all the time, even when you are sleeping, to keep you alive and healthy. It's an amazing muscle! When someone has a 'cardiac crisis,' it means their heart needs extra help from doctors. The doctors at the hospital are doing their very best to make her heart strong again.

How Hospitals Help People

When we go to a hospital, doctors and nurses are there to take care of us. They give us medicine, check our temperature, and make sure we are comfortable. They have special tools to look inside our bodies and understand why we are sick. They work very hard to make sure everyone gets better and can go home feeling healthy again. It's a good thing we have hospitals and kind doctors and nurses all over the world, including right here in Africa, to help people when they are not well.

Why This Matters to Us

Narges Mohammadi is a brave lady who cares a lot about others. Even when she is unwell, her message of peace and fairness is important. It reminds us all to be kind to each other. It reminds us to help our friends and family when they are sad or sick. It reminds us to try and make our homes, schools, and countries peaceful places. Even kids can be peace heroes! You can be kind to your friends, share your toys, and help people who need it. That's a great way to make our world better.

Sending Our Good Wishes

We are all hoping that Narges Mohammadi gets better very, very soon. We send her big, warm wishes from all of us here in Africa. We hope the doctors and nurses help her feel strong and healthy again. Remember to always be kind, be brave, and help others when you can. That's how we make the world a better place, just like Narges Mohammadi tries to do every day. Stay healthy and keep shining, everyone! Bye for now!

originalTitle: Trump tells Congress ceasefire means he does not need their approval for Iran war
title: Trump tells Congress ceasefire means he does not need their approval for Iran war
content: The president writes that hostilities "have terminated" because of the ceasefire, arguing he does not need congressional authorisation.

originalTitle: Billions of meals at risk due to Iran war, says fertiliser boss
title: Billions of meals at risk due to Iran war, says fertiliser boss
content: A shortage of fertiliser due to the Iran conflict could reduce crop yields and push prices higher, says the boss of Yara.

originalTitle: French PM fuels row with trip to buy baguettes
title: French PM fuels row with trip to buy baguettes
content: Sébastien Lecornu visited a boulangerie on Labour Day, defying unions who say 1 May must remain a mandatory rest day.

originalTitle: 'It's like we're all crying and relegated' - Spurs' De Zerbi delivers monologue
title: 'It's like we're all crying and relegated' - Spurs' De Zerbi delivers monologue
content: Manager Roberto de Zerbi urges Tottenham to "silence the voice inside of us" as the club battle to avoid relegation from the Premier League.

originalTitle: Leeds beat Burnley to move closer to safety
title: Leeds beat Burnley to move closer to safety
content: Leeds move nine points clear of the relegation places with a home victory over managerless Burnley.

originalTitle: Saudi Arabian owners' desire for Newcastle 'unchanged'
title: Saudi Arabian owners' desire for Newcastle 'unchanged'
content: Head coach Eddie Howe says Newcastle's owners remain "ambitious" for the club despite the Saudi Arabian PIF's decision to pull out of funding LIV Golf.
